dataRequirements.description: The CSV file produced from records containing CSV-significant characters, used for round-trip validation.

provenance.rationale: Producing a valid CSV intrinsically requires correct escaping of significant characters even though escaping rules are unstated (OQ-csv-format).

dataRequirements.description: At least two user accounts, each owning distinct identifiable records, to verify export scoping.
dataRequirements.name: Two users with distinct attributable records

provenance.rationale: Evidence scopes export to a user's own data, requiring a multi-user fixture to prove scoping.

dataRequirements.description: The CSV file produced from a high-volume dataset, checked for completeness and no truncation.
dataRequirements.name: Exported CSV file (large volume)

provenance.rationale: Large export output is assumed to scale to the full dataset until volume and delivery limits are confirmed (OQ-csv-volume, OQ-csv-delivery).

dataRequirements.description: The CSV file produced by the export operation containing the user's stored records.
dataRequirements.name: Exported CSV file (basic)

dataRequirements.description: The CSV file produced for the requesting user in a multi-user environment, inspected for foreign records.
dataRequirements.name: Exported CSV file (scoping check)

provenance.rationale: Output of the scoped export, which evidence requires to contain only the requesting user's data.

dataRequirements.description: A user account holding at least one record of exportable data, with an active authenticated session.
dataRequirements.name: Authenticated user with exportable records

provenance.rationale: Evidence states users export their data, which presupposes a user account holding data.

dataRequirements.description: A user account with an active session but zero exportable records.

provenance.rationale: Empty-dataset boundary derives from the export capability though empty-state output is undefined (OQ-csv-format).

evidence.claim: Users can export their data as a CSV file.
evidence.excerpt: Users can export their data as a CSV file.

features.description: Allows users to export their data out of the system in a portable file format, starting with CSV.
features.name: Data Export

provenance.rationale: REQ-csv-export concerns exporting user data to a file; grouped under a Data Export feature derived directly from the stated CSV export capability.

targets.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

warnings: [critical] Missing IDOR/authorization scenario: no case where an authenticated user attempts to export ANOTHER user's data by manipulating the scope/identifier in the request. OQ-csv-access explicitly raises 'can a user export another user's data', but only implicit ownership scoping (TC-csv-export-scoping-own-data-only, observing one export) is tested. For a PII export with an undefined access model, the active cross-user request attempt is the highest-risk surface and is uncovered. This is material — plan is incomplete on security coverage.

[warning] Provenance over-claim on scoping. TC-csv-export-scoping-own-data-only and assertions AS-scope-no-foreign-records / AS-scope-contains-own-records are marked provenance.kind 'explicit', but the sole evidence ('users can export their data as a CSV file') does not state that ownership scoping is enforced. Enforcement is an inference, not an explicit claim — these should be 'inferred', consistent with OQ-csv-access/OQ-csv-scope still being open.

[warning] Priority inconsistency: TC-csv-export-scoping-own-data-only is p0 while its only covered requirement REQ-csv-export is p1. A p0 test cannot exceed the priority of its requirement without justification; either raise REQ-csv-export or reconcile the case priority.

[warning] Weak/possibly-wrong expected outcomes on empty-dataset case. AS-empty-valid-csv (conformsToSchema) and AS-empty-zero-rows (count=0) assume header-only output, yet OQ-csv-format is unresolved and automation.readiness is 'blocked'. If the defined empty-state behaviour turns out to be an error, an empty file, or a 'no data' message, these assertions will false-fail. Expected results should be flagged as contingent on OQ-csv-format, not asserted as fixed.

[warning] Missing failure/error-path coverage: no case for export job failure, partial failure mid-export, timeout handling beyond the large-volume happy path, or retry/idempotency. For an async export model (assumed in steps), failure handling is a standard expected path and is absent.

[warning] Missing delivery/expiry coverage despite OQ-csv-delivery. No case validates the delivery mechanism (synchronous download vs emailed/async link) or download-link expiry behaviour, even though steps assume an async completion-and-retrieve flow with a 30s wait.

[info] Breadth exceeds confirmed scope: six cases plus performance, security, and format assertions are derived from a single one-line evidence statement. Most are honestly marked 'inferred'/'assumption' and gated on open questions, which is acceptable — but the plan is largely speculative and cannot be executed/validated until the two blocking questions (OQ-csv-scope, OQ-csv-access) are resolved. Four of six cases are automation 'blocked' or 'partial'.

[info] Invented permission token 'export-own-data' is used across actors and presupposes a permission model that is unconfirmed (OQ-csv-access). Reasonable as an assumption but should be surfaced as such rather than treated as established.

provenance.rationale: Delivery mechanism affects the user flow and test design but is not described in the evidence.

openQuestions.question: How is the export delivered — synchronous download, emailed link, or async job with notification — and is there a download expiry?

provenance.rationale: CSV correctness depends on format conventions that the evidence does not specify.

openQuestions.question: What are the required CSV formatting rules (delimiter, encoding, header row, quoting, date/number formats, locale handling)?

provenance.rationale: "Their data" implies ownership scoping and access control that must be confirmed to prevent data exposure.

openQuestions.question: Who is authorized to trigger an export, and can a user only export their own data versus another user's or organization-wide data?

provenance.rationale: The evidence references "their data" without defining the dataset, fields, or boundaries needed to validate export completeness.

openQuestions.question: What exactly does "their data" include — which entities, fields, and date ranges are in scope for export?

provenance.rationale: Large exports commonly hit performance and size limits; assuming this matters until scale is confirmed.

openQuestions.question: What is the expected maximum data volume per export, and are there performance, pagination, or row-limit constraints?

provenance.rationale: Stated directly in evidence: users can export their data as a CSV file.

requirements.statement: The system shall allow users to export their data as a CSV file.

steps.description: As authenticated user A in a multi-user environment, trigger a CSV export of own data.

provenance.rationale: Evidence requires export to be scoped to the requesting user's data.

provenance.rationale: Parsing the file confirms it is valid CSV with no data rows (OQ-csv-format).

testCases.objective: Verify an authenticated user's export contains exclusively their own records and no other users' data.

postconditions.description: Exported CSV contains only records belonging to the requesting user and none belonging to other users.
postconditions.expectedState: export strictly scoped to requesting user

preconditions.description: At least two users exist, each with distinct identifiable records.

provenance.rationale: Evidence states users export 'their data', so the export must be scoped to the requesting user.

testCases.riskRationale: Data scoping is the highest-impact failure mode; cross-user leakage is a serious data-privacy breach. Scope is open (OQ-csv-scope, OQ-csv-access).

target.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

testCases.title: Export returns only the requesting user's own data

testCases.objective: Verify exporting with zero records yields a valid CSV (header only or defined empty-state behaviour) rather than an error or malformed file.

postconditions.description: Export completes without error and the resulting file parses as valid CSV.
postconditions.expectedState: valid CSV with no data rows

preconditions.description: User account exists with no exportable records.

provenance.rationale: Empty-dataset boundary follows from the export capability though the empty-state output is not defined in evidence.

testCases.riskRationale: Empty-state handling is unspecified (OQ-csv-format); ambiguous behaviour can produce corrupt files or confusing UX.

target.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

testCases.title: Export when user has no data returns an empty-but-valid CSV

blockers: Export format and column scope undefined (OQ-csv-format, OQ-csv-scope)

testCases.objective: Verify an authenticated user can trigger a data export and receive a well-formed CSV file containing their data.

postconditions.description: A CSV file is delivered to the user containing rows that match the user's stored data.

preconditions.description: User account exists and holds at least one record of exportable data.

provenance.rationale: Directly validates the stated capability that users can export their data as a CSV file.

testCases.riskRationale: Core happy-path of the only stated capability; export format and scope are unconfirmed (OQ-csv-scope, OQ-csv-format), so a defect here breaks the primary user value.

target.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

testCases.title: Export user data as CSV produces a downloadable CSV file

testCases.objective: Verify an anonymous request to export data is rejected and no CSV is produced.

postconditions.description: Export is denied and no CSV file is generated or returned.

provenance.rationale: Evidence scopes export to 'users' but does not state access controls; we assume authentication is required pending OQ-csv-access.

testCases.riskRationale: Export access controls are unconfirmed (OQ-csv-access); allowing anonymous export would leak user data.

target.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

testCases.title: Unauthenticated user cannot trigger a data export

testCases.objective: Verify data containing CSV-significant characters is escaped per RFC 4180 so the file parses without column misalignment or data loss.

postconditions.description: Re-parsing the exported CSV yields the exact original field values with correct column alignment.

preconditions.description: User dataset includes values with commas, double quotes, and embedded newlines.

provenance.rationale: Field escaping is intrinsic to producing a valid CSV file even though the evidence does not state escaping rules.

testCases.riskRationale: Improper escaping silently corrupts exported data and undermines the integrity guarantee of the export; format spec is open (OQ-csv-format).

target.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

testCases.title: CSV export correctly escapes commas, quotes, and newlines in field values

blockers: Volume limits and timeout/delivery model undefined (OQ-csv-volume, OQ-csv-delivery)

testCases.objective: Verify a user with a high record count can export all data without truncation, timeout, or memory failure.

postconditions.description: Exported CSV row count equals the user's total record count with no truncation.

preconditions.description: User dataset contains a large number of records near the expected upper bound.

provenance.rationale: No volume bounds are stated; we assume exports must scale to a user's full dataset until OQ-csv-volume is resolved.

testCases.riskRationale: Export volume limits are undefined (OQ-csv-volume); large exports risk timeouts, truncation, or resource exhaustion.

target.description: CSV export capability enabling users to download their data as a CSV file.

testCases.title: Export of a large dataset completes successfully within acceptable limits
